name: Web Theme
description: Standard for the concrete-value layer of a design system — the primitive scales (spacing, radius, elevation, type ramp, color palette) and per-component overrides that the semantic Web Intents (density, surface, typography, motion, theme-color) resolve into. Authored and interchanged as DTCG 2025.10 (the stable W3C design-token format — the one legitimate Protocol here, adopted not coined) and compiled to the native CSS runtime: custom properties + @property, with light-dark()/color-scheme for schemes and relative-color/color-mix() for derived accent scales, validated against WCAG/APCA per step. Ships a complete default token set; a consuming project overrides via extends rather than authoring from scratch. A 3-tier taxonomy (primitive · semantic · component) where the semantic tier stays owned by the intents — no parallel vocabulary. Distinct from Web Intents (the UX-“what”): Web Theme supplies the values that “what” points into.
tierEvidence: benchmarkCoverage covers design-tokens-theming (#364 graduatedTo project:webtheme); the DTCG token format is the design-system tokens standard every benchmark DS ships. FUI ships the compiler (webtheme/compile.ts, defaultTokens.ts).
